description: Canonical service onboarding procedures for already-registered DevGov services. Machine-local paths and transient probe evidence stay in reports/.

entries.healthProcedure: Use /health on the governed dashboard origin.
entries.resetProcedure: Use doctor:repair only for regenerating local reports; dashboard restart/reset execution remains separate from Doctor.
entries.startupProcedure: Use scripts/open-dashboard.mjs for on-demand start and startup registry entries for reviewed login startup.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status row is already surfaced as devgov-dashboard plus public-route rows.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: Use the DevGov public route registry and reviewed tunnel startup scripts for gov/dev hostnames.
entries.nextAction: Keep as reference implementation for the remaining service onboarding records.
entries.notes: This is the completed baseline service-control pattern.

entries.doctorProcedure: Use docs/APP_SERVER_SERVICE.md headless validation as evidence today; add a stable target Doctor wrapper for service state, token-file presence, queue health, and log freshness before marking Doctor FOUND.
entries.resetProcedure: REVIEW_REQUIRED: service restart, queue reset, token repair, or WinSW reinstall must remain operator-reviewed and must not be executed from the dashboard.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status already has the local-agent row; update Doctor to FOUND after the project Doctor is registered.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No public Cloudflare route is registered; keep loopback-only unless a future route review is approved.
entries.nextAction: Add a target Doctor command that wraps the existing start-app-server health gate and APP_SERVER_SERVICE headless validation, then promote its stable ref into DevGov.
entries.notes: Bearer token paths, service home, logs, and generated indexes must remain out of canonical registry data.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the project Doctor in read-only mode to confirm PS3 Eye USB presence, DirectShow input availability, frame freshness, virtual-camera registration, and capture viability.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use ps3eye-windows-virtual-camera/scripts/Test-Ps3EyeCameraDoctor.ps1 as the stable diagnostic candidate; keep repair mode operator-reviewed until restart, feeder, and virtual-camera boundaries are documented.
entries.resetProcedure: REVIEW_REQUIRED: stopping or starting VCamSample, ffmpeg feeder processes, virtual-camera registration, or camera-device ownership must remain explicit and observable.
entries.startupProcedure: Default policy is on-demand and login startup disabled; keep ps3eye-system-camera-login as a candidate only for reviewed opt-in service mode.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Do not expose dashboard restart yet; surface as a service-onboarding candidate after a safe one-shot camera health contract is added.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No Cloudflare route applies; camera observation must stay local-only and must not expose images or device streams through public routes.
entries.nextAction: Add a small on-demand capture health contract for this service, then decide whether PS3 Eye ffmpeg bridging remains the durable path or is replaced by direct C++ capture.
entries.notes: The current PS3 Eye bridge needs long-lived ffmpeg/feeder state when enabled; do not register it as an always-on service for Codex observation without a separate privacy and resource review.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the registered public /api/health route plus the runtime local health target written by scripts/runtime/windows-persistent-server.ps1.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the DevGov Doctor wrapper to check the runtime status script, loopback 127.0.0.1:4321 health, and public staging /api/health together.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov restart wrapper to ensure the local runtime is healthy on 127.0.0.1:4321, while keeping any public route mismatch visible until the tunnel target is fixed.
entries.startupProcedure: Keep startup review-gated through codex-calendar-todo/scripts/windows-persistent-server.ps1 and runtime scripts.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status public-route row uses approved Doctor and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: Use the registered staging-private route; confirm Access and runtime health together before approval.
entries.nextAction: Reconcile the public Cloudflare route target from 127.0.0.1:9190 to the actual runtime authority on 127.0.0.1:4321, then re-run staging health checks.
entries.notes: The startup target is a runtime project; do not point login startup at the development worktree.

entries.healthProcedure: Registered DevGov route currently uses /health; the external access plan evidence uses /healthz and /readyz, so reconcile the durable health contract before approval.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use the external access plan checklist as evidence today; create a Doctor record that checks local listener, tunnel ingress validation, public /healthz and /readyz endpoints, and startup persistence without exposing credentials.
entries.resetProcedure: REVIEW_REQUIRED: app-server restart, tunnel restart, token repair, or ingress changes must be checked locally first and must not expose credentials or logs.
entries.startupProcedure: Keep restart review-gated through codex/Start-CodexRemoteOnLogin.ps1 until command boundaries are reviewed.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status already has the public-route row; reconcile endpoint naming before approval.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: Use the dedicated Cloudflare Tunnel route for codex-remote.colorgeek.co; validate ingress and Access posture before probing public /healthz and /readyz.
entries.nextAction: Confirm whether /health, /healthz, or /readyz is the durable DevGov health contract, then update public-route healthUrl and Doctor procedure together.
entries.notes: The durable owner is a runtime plan rather than a normal source repository.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the public health URLs and the TB2 local MCP startup/status scripts.
entries.doctorProcedure: Promote TB2 status-runtime and verify-runtime checks into a stable Doctor reference.
entries.resetProcedure: REVIEW_REQUIRED: local MCP restart and runtime sync require an explicit operator-approved sequence.
entries.startupProcedure: Register a reviewed TB2 start-local-mcp procedure before dashboard restart can be considered.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status has public-route rows but lacks Doctor and restart readiness.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: Use the approved public-health Cloudflare route records; do not expose MCP execution through public health routes.
entries.nextAction: Map the TB2 Windows entrypoint scripts to stable DevGov refs without copying WSL/source-local details.
entries.notes: The Windows entrypoint keeps source and runtime boundaries separate.

entries.healthProcedure: Use runtime:check and the registered public route health target.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use scripts/check-runtime.mjs as the stable runtime Doctor for local and public reachability.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov Taste restart wrapper to ensure the local Next.js runtime is listening, then keep any remaining tunnel or auth failures visible for follow-up.
entries.startupProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov on-demand ensure-running wrapper for 127.0.0.1:3010; no login startup automation is registered yet.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status public-route row uses approved Taste Doctor and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: Keep the public route mapped to the governed loopback listener and align tunnel docs before changes.
entries.nextAction: Decide later whether Taste also needs reviewed login startup automation beyond the current on-demand control path.
entries.notes: The public listener must remain governed by 127.0.0.1:3010 and the reviewed tunnel route.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the registered protected public route only as reachability evidence until a local app health contract is confirmed.
entries.doctorProcedure: Create a DevGov-owned external-app Doctor checklist for listener state, model API readiness, and Access exposure.
entries.resetProcedure: REVIEW_REQUIRED: external app restart is not known safe until the launcher or service owner is confirmed.
entries.startupProcedure: Identify the LM Studio startup owner before registering any start or restart reference.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Service Status has the public-route row; owner and startup remain unresolved.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: Keep Cloudflare Access required and confirm whether this route should remain publicly reachable.
entries.nextAction: Confirm whether LM Studio is manually started, service-managed, or launcher-managed.
entries.notes: Treat as protected local model API until exposure policy is reviewed.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the local preview root and /api/shaderglass/status after the server is started.
entries.doctorProcedure: Add a preview Doctor that checks governed port preflight, static preview availability, and shader bridge status.
entries.resetProcedure: REVIEW_REQUIRED: reset may only clear preview runtime state or restart the local preview, never calibration state.
entries.startupProcedure: Register a reviewed local preview start command; keep calibration and preview boundaries explicit.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Add a dashboard service-status mapping after a health URL is registered.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No Cloudflare route is approved; keep this preview local-only.
entries.nextAction: Convert the PORTS.md verification block into a stable Doctor/start procedure.
entries.notes: This is a preview service, not a calibration authority.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the local http://127.0.0.1:3287/health endpoint described in SBS PORTS.md and local proxy docs.
entries.doctorProcedure: Use scripts/proxy-doctor.mjs as the stable SBS local proxy Doctor.
entries.resetProcedure: Use the reviewed DevGov SBS restart wrapper, which runs proxy-doctor in start mode and keeps any backend warnings visible.
entries.startupProcedure: Use scripts/start-proxy-protocol.ps1 and proxy:doctor:start as the reviewed on-demand ensure-running path; no login startup entry is required.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Surface SBS as a local service target with approved Health, Doctor, and Restart controls through the local-only DevGov control listener.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No Cloudflare route is approved; keep the extension proxy loopback-only.
entries.nextAction: Decide later whether LM Studio backend readiness should become a separate DevGov-managed local service row.
entries.notes: This service already has strong project-local proxy health documentation.

entries.healthProcedure: Run: tunnel-client health --url http://127.0.0.1:8080/readyz --json and confirm readiness.
entries.doctorProcedure: Run: openai-mcp-tunnel/scripts/tunnel-client/tunnel-client-runtime.ps1 -Mode Doctor. Doctor checks plugin-hint presence and profile mount path coverage.
entries.resetProcedure: REVIEW_REQUIRED: stop/restart or runtime override should be explicit before automation.
entries.startupProcedure: Run: openai-mcp-tunnel/scripts/tunnel-client/register-tunnel-client-startup.ps1 to enable startup; runtime start uses openai-mcp-tunnel/scripts/tunnel-client/tunnel-client-runtime.ps1 -Mode QuickStart.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Keep service-status row as candidate until health and doctor checks complete end-to-end.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No public route. Keep local-only unless a reviewed public route is added.
entries.nextAction: Collect one completed health + doctor + startup evidence report and promote after validation.
entries.notes: MCP command mounts reviewed local workspace and model-library roots.

entries.healthProcedure: Use the local Vite root after strict governed startup is added.
entries.doctorProcedure: Create a lightweight Doctor around lint, test, and governed dev-server preflight.
entries.resetProcedure: REVIEW_REQUIRED: reset may clean dev-server state only through a reviewed wrapper, not arbitrary project cleanup.
entries.startupProcedure: Wrap dev startup with require-governed-port before binding 127.0.0.1:3100.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Add a dashboard service-status mapping after the health URL and startup wrapper exist.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No Cloudflare route is approved for the dev server; keep local-only.
entries.nextAction: Update target project dev script or add a reviewed wrapper in a later apply batch.
entries.notes: The root project and nested app currently expose similar package scripts; confirm the active source root before applying.

entries.healthProcedure: No reviewed health endpoint is confirmed yet.
entries.doctorProcedure: Create a project-owner discovery procedure before adding Doctor or startup refs.
entries.resetProcedure: REVIEW_REQUIRED: do not define reset until the owner, data boundaries, and control panel command are confirmed.
entries.startupProcedure: Do not register startup until the source owner and control panel command are confirmed.
entries.dashboardProcedure: Do not add a dashboard status row until the owner and health URL are confirmed.
entries.cloudflareProcedure: No Cloudflare route is approved; keep local-only until the owner is confirmed.
entries.nextAction: Locate the actual video-render-kit source or retire the registry entry if it was only a planning placeholder.
entries.notes: Nearby video repos did not confirm ownership of the 127.0.0.1:8765 control panel.
